summary: Ronacher opens a series by arguing that building a custom web framework can be more practical than adopting a stock one, because real applications often need behavior that mainstream frameworks won’t prioritize. He recounts how modifying Django for a past project led to a forked codebase that became hard to upgrade and ultimately divergent. A bespoke framework, he argues, keeps you flexible and lets you understand and fix everything yourself. He then demonstrates a minimal Flask-like framework built on Werkzeug, showing the core routing and request/response flow. The conclusion is that a small, comprehensible framework is both achievable and a solid foundation for learning how full frameworks work.
key_points: Custom frameworks can be more flexible than off-the-shelf options when application needs diverge from mainstream priorities.
Modifying a large framework creates upgrade pain and can trap you in a maintenance fork.
A minimal framework can be built by combining Werkzeug’s routing, request/response objects, and a WSGI callable.
Passing the request explicitly keeps the design simple and makes the machinery easier to understand.
Understanding WSGI and Werkzeug’s helpers demystifies how larger frameworks operate under the hood.
money_quotes: It is quite unpopular these days to go with building your own framework; everybody quickly shouts "reinventing the wheel" and points you to one of the tons of existing web frameworks out there.
But it is actually a really good idea to create a framework for an application and not go with a stock one.
Turns out: Django like every other framework out there is improving quickly, but often not in the areas you might be interested in.
Another reason to roll your own framework is that you know everything and you can fix it quickly yourself.
Looks a lot like a simplified Flask version, which is exactly what it should be.
So what we created is a WSGI application.
key_insight: Building a small, custom framework atop Werkzeug can be more adaptable and educational than bending a large framework to your will.
